page: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with 614 GB of capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark results place the E16-8ds_v4 consistently in the average tier for most operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is measured at 1415.19, while multi-core performance reaches 7440.93. Memory bandwidth is robust, with read-only small block cached speeds at 415.26 GB/sec and large block uncached read speeds at 49.07 GB/sec. In database-centric tests, the server handles 7.04 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 2904.22 on PassMark database operations. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 27.55 GB/sec, and medium-sized LLM inference is poor, yielding 14.81 tokens/sec for prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16-8ds_v4 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without paying for unnecessary vCPUs. The primary tradeoff is its moderate CPU performance, as shown by average Geekbench scores, which limits its suitability for compute-heavy tasks like large-scale LLM inference. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and moderate web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs clocked at 2.6 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and 614 GB of local storage. The hardware configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 4889.0 and 7.04 million Redis SET operations per second. However, compute-heavy tasks such as medium-sized LLM inference show weaker performance. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that do not require massive parallel compute, making it highly suitable for database hosting, in-memory caching, and general memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 614 GB of local storage, designed for database hosting and in-memory caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with high RAM density and local storage, built for databases and in-memory caching workloads.

128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory density
614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
7.04 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for database hosting, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive workloads
